title: Acridine Orange Staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"52vt5","text":"Please contact Dr. Steven Wilhelm (wilhelm@utk.edu) for additional information regarding this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c1m1p","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8cbj2","text":"Modified from the Handbook of Methods in Aquatic Microbial Ecology (p. 220)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Form a meniscus with Milli-Q H 2 O (used with freshwater samples) or ESAW (saltwater samples) on the vacuum round
Place 0.45 µm HAWP filter on meniscus and turn on vacuum to ~5 mmHg
Make another bubble with Milli-Q H 2 O or ESAW on the filter
Place a 0.2 µm GTBP filter onto the bubble
Place the filter tower over the filter
Pour 2 mL of a preserved water sample (or a sample which has been diluted to 2 mL with Milli-Q or ESAW) onto the filter
Add 10 µL of 1% w/v filtered Acridine Orange (AO) and mix Acridine orange Fisher Scientific Catalog #AC423340010
Keep the AO in a dark place when not using it, and use it under low light conditions
Vacuum and rinse with ~2 mL Milli-Q H 2 O or ESAW when the meniscus of the sample reaches the filter surface
When the entire sample has been filtered, remove the filter tower

Remove the membrane filter while still under a vacuum and place onto a prepared slide To prepare a slide, place 5 small drops of immersion oil on a cover slip and one drop on the slide. Place the cover slip on the slide. Before laying the filter on the slide, lift off the cover slip.
Replace the cover slip and remove any air bubbles
View the slides with the Leica DM4000-6000 epifluorescent microscope using the L5 (blue) fillter at excitation = 482 nm and emission = 525 nm
creator: Steven W Wilhelm
